title: Validity Mirage: Context Compression Failure Modes in LLMs
description:
Research on context compression failure modes in large language models. Introduces the validity mirage — where compression preserves surface-level correctness while silently substituting the governing hypothesis — and develops a tropical semiring algebra (max-plus over R ∪ {−∞}) for measuring context health. The public site foregrounds a deterministic replay witness (n=3 per policy and retention fraction) and raw validation logs mirrored from the companion tropical-mcp evaluation repo; broader model, streaming, and real-incident counts are discussed in the paper bundle rather than treated as site-headline witness metrics.

Included paper bundle:
- paper_00_continuous_control_structural_regularization.pdf
- paper_01_absorbing_states_in_greedy_search.pdf
- paper_02_streaming_oscillation_traps.pdf
- paper_03_validity_mirage_compression.pdf
- paper_i_tropical_algebra.pdf

Paper artifacts are intended for CC-BY 4.0 scholarly distribution.
